chunk_summary: This section emphasizes linking clinical stunting care to public nutrition programs in India, ensuring continuity of care through documentation, program referrals, and monitoring tools like Poshan Tracker.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of linking stunting care to public nutrition programs like Mission POSHAN 2.0 and PM POSHAN.
Learn how to use Poshan Tracker for growth monitoring and validate clinic measurements.
Identify barriers to program access for children and adolescents, including stigma, attendance, and meal quality.
key_facts: Stunting care should integrate public platforms like anganwadi centers and PM POSHAN for food and nutrition delivery.
Poshan Tracker is a governance tool for growth monitoring; clinicians should validate abnormal measurements in clinics.
National Deworming Day schedules and vitamin A rounds are critical for adolescent health and anemia prevention.
Documentation templates should include phenotype, determinant barriers, and program actions for auditable follow-up.
Anemia platforms target both school-going and out-of-school adolescents, including married adolescent girls.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: The Poshan Tracker is used for growth monitoring governance; clinicians can add value by validating abnormal measurements in clinic and reinforcing correct technique.
document_key_topics: Linkage to public nutrition programs in India
chunk_heading: STUNTING, CHRONIC UNDERNUTRITION & DETERMINANTS;INDIA PROGRAM LINKAGE AND CLINIC DOCUMENTATION FOR CONTINUITY OF CARE;
